content.full_text:
Lazarus & Saunders Incorporated
AN ECONOMIC AND FINANCIAL CONSULTING CORPORATION
3600 WILSHIRE BOULEVARD, SUITE 2030
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90010
TELEPHONE: (213) 389-1383

JERRY H. LAZARUS
RUSSELL L. SAUNDERS
STUART I. SHAW

February 7, 1978

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California    95804

To Whom it May Concern:

I am the accountant for a Non-profit association which was
originated many years ago, primarily for the advancement
and promotion of compatible relations between the members of
the association, other groups and associations and governmental
agencies identified with their profession. It was formed
pursuant to the General Non-profit Corporation Law of the
State of California and funds are raised by dues and assessments.

This association now desires to make political contributions
to candidates that support its views, whom are running for
statewide office. In connection with this, the following
questions have been raised:

1. Will the association retain its non-profit status?
2. Can members still take a deduction on their tax
   return for dues and assessments paid to the
   association?
3. If contributions made by the association for a
   calendar year are under $5,000.00, is it true that
   no reports are required?
4. If the contributions exceed $5,000.00, what reports
   are necessary?
5. If a political action committee is formed and
   basically controlled by the association, is it true
   that the reporting requirements are more demanding?

I would appreciate your answers as soon as possible. If you
have any questions regarding this matter, please feel free to
contact me.

Yours truly,

Stewart Shaw, Los Angeles. He was advised by phone (February 15,
1978 and February 21, 1978) that the association and/or its
related PAC will be a recipient committee depending upon how
they raise and spend money for political purposes. He was sent
TA materials and Regulations 18215, 18225 and 18419. He was
referred to FTB for questions regarding taxes.

sections.question_synthetic: A non-profit association seeking to make political contributions to statewide candidates asks whether it will retain non-profit status, whether member dues remain tax-deductible, what reporting requirements apply based on contribution amounts, and whether forming a related PAC increases reporting obligations.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response letter. The document shows informal telephone advice was provided to Stewart Shaw on February 15 and 21, 1978, but no formal written conclusion or advice letter is present in this document. The OCR quality is poor in some sections, particularly the header area.

embedding.summary: An accountant for a non-profit association submits questions to the FPPC regarding the association's ability to make political contributions while maintaining non-profit status, tax treatment of member dues, and applicable reporting requirements under California political finance law.
